title: Pteroxygonum denticulatum
intro: Pteroxygonum denticulatum, commonly known as the toothed pteroxygonum, is a flowering plant species native to East Asia, specifically in China and Japan, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and toothed leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Pteroxygonum denticulatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, with the dried roots and rhizomes being the most commonly used parts for herbal preparations.
taxonomy: Pteroxygonum denticulatum is classified within the family Polygonaceae. It belongs to the order Polygalales, and the class Magnoliopsida. The order Polygalales and the family Polygonaceae are part of the larger clade of flowering plants known as angiosperms. The class Magnoliopsida is a subgroup of the larger clade of dicotyledons. Pteroxygonum denticulatum's classification at the rank of kingdom is within the kingdom Plantae.

It is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and reduce swelling.
